patches.hypothesis: The original patch removed the handling of the ANNOTATION case, which caused overfitting by not allowing annotations to be part of the extracted text block when appropriate. The refined fix reintroduces the ANNOTATION case and modifies the logic to append text until an EOC, EOF, or another annotation is encountered, ensuring annotations are correctly processed and included in the extracted text.
patches.original_hypothesis: The issue arises because the method stops processing when it encounters an annotation, even though it should continue reading until an EOC, EOF, or another annotation is encountered. The fix involves modifying the switch statement to handle annotations differently, allowing it to continue appending text until the end condition is met.

patches.hypothesis: The root cause of the issue is that the current patch appends annotations to the extracted text, which is not always the desired behavior. We need to introduce a mechanism to differentiate between annotations that should be included in the extracted text and those that should terminate the extraction process. This can be done by checking the type of annotation and deciding whether to include it or stop the extraction.
patches.original_hypothesis: The issue arises because the method does not correctly handle the transition from one line to another when encountering an annotation. Specifically, it stops processing further lines once it encounters an annotation, which causes the '@author' part to be omitted.
